ddls:
CREATE TABLE "maps" (
  "map_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each map */,
  "map_name" TEXT /* Name of the map */,
  "terrain_type" TEXT /* Type of terrain (e.g., lowtown, forest, desert) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the map */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the map was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the map */,
  "map_size" REAL /* Size of the map in square units */,
  "complexity" INTEGER /* Measure of the map's complexity */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the map */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("map_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maps_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "scenarios" (
  "scen_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each scenario */,
  "scen_name" TEXT /* Name of the scenario */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the scenario */,
  "map_id" TEXT /* Reference to the map the scenario is associated with */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the scenario was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the scenario */,
  "difficulty_level" TEXT /* Measure of the scenario's difficulty */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the scenario */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("scen_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scenarios_map_id FOREIGN KEY ("map_id") REFERENCES maps ("map_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scenarios_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pathfind_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pathfinding result */,
  "scen_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the scenario the result is associated with */,
  "tcost" REAL /* Total cost of the path */,
  "distance" REAL /* Distance of the path */,
  "expect" REAL /* Expected value of the path */,
  "steps" INTEGER /* Number of steps in the path */,
  "d" INTEGER /* Direction (if applicable, -1 for unspecified) */,
  "itype" TEXT /* Type of iteration or pathfinding algorithm used */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the pathfinding result was generated */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the result */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the pathfinding result */,
  "execution_time" REAL /* Time taken to compute the path */,
  "algorithm_version" TEXT /* Version of the pathfinding algorithm used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pathfind_results_itype FOREIGN KEY ("itype") REFERENCES iterations ("itype_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pathfind_results_scen_id FOREIGN KEY ("scen_id") REFERENCES scenarios ("scen_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "iterations" (
  "itype_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each iteration type */,
  "itype_name" TEXT /* Name of the iteration type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the iteration type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the iteration type was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the iteration type */,
  "optimization_level" TEXT /* Measure of the optimization level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("itype_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, tester, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the pathfinding result or scenario being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client making the access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES pathfind_results ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
